rationale: The cited paper is discussed in the hypothesis-development section, not just listed as background. The citing authors summarize Dhar and Wertenbroch's vice-virtue mixed-set findings and directly use them to motivate a moderator prediction about category heterogeneity. This is a meaningful conceptual borrowing for the citing paper's framework, even though the main empirical setting and methods differ.
relation_description: Nikolova and Inman use Dhar and Wertenbroch (2012) as theoretical support for their argument that mixed healthy-unhealthy assortments create self-signaling utility when shoppers choose healthier options. They apply that logic to predict a stronger POS nutrition-score effect in categories with higher within-category nutrition-score variability (H3).
